title: Fix terminal.ts permission-ATTN vs idle-ATTN distinction

tasks.title: Add failing regression tests for permission-ATTN (immediate ring) vs idle-ATTN (debounce/armed) distinction.
tasks.verification: Regression tests updated: ATTN always rings immediately, IDLE uses armed/debounce. TODO comment removed. Committed in worktree.

tasks.implementation_summary: Regression tests updated: ATTN always rings immediately, IDLE uses armed/debounce. TODO comment removed. Committed in worktree.

tasks.summary: Implemented: separated ATTN (permission, always immediate) from IDLE (armed/debounce) in updateTerminalStatus bell logic.
tasks.implementation_summary: Implemented: separated ATTN (permission, always immediate) from IDLE (armed/debounce) in updateTerminalStatus bell logic.

tasks.title: Run focused terminal tests and verify bell behavior.

proposal.approval_evidence: Proposal created from issue #86 TODO comment. Clear scope: distinguish permission-ATTN from idle-ATTN in terminal.ts. No HITL tradeoff.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery from issue #86 and TODO comment in events.test.ts:712. Well-scoped terminal.ts fix. No HITL tradeoff.

design.approval_evidence: Design: separate ATTN (permission pending) from IDLE (agent finished) in bell logic. ATTN always rings immediately. IDLE uses armed/debounce.

planning.approval_evidence: Planning complete: 3 tasks (validation, implementation, verification). ATTN=permission rings immediately. IDLE=agent-finished uses armed/debounce.

execution.approval_evidence: All 3 tasks done. ATTN (permission) always rings immediately. IDLE (agent-finished) uses armed/debounce. TODO removed. Typecheck + lint clean. All tests pass.

acceptance.approval_evidence: Batch user approval. All 3 tasks done: ATTN=permission rings immediately, IDLE=agent-finished uses armed/debounce. TODO removed. All tests pass.

release.approval_evidence: All gates complete. Ready to archive.

clarify_findings.message: Proposal references authentication/authorization without specifying the model. What auth mechanism will be used?
Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
